contradictions.claim_text: The same model can be applied to France, Belgium and Switzerland without material redesign.
contradictions.notes: EU, France, Belgium, and Switzerland sources show materially different recognition and language-proof mechanics. A single undifferentiated route story is not source-safe.

open_issues.claim_text: The product's certificate will be accepted for practice or recognition.
open_issues.notes: Belgium explicitly rejects course-attendance proof, and no rerun source validates provider-issued certificate acceptance in France, Belgium, or Switzerland.
No current primary France source was added to support the number.

open_issues.claim_text: No dedicated online Medical French provider identified.
open_issues.notes: Some substitute online/distance language provision is publicly visible, but the rerun did not perform a complete market census for dedicated medical French.

open_issues.claim_text: France's public guidance for regulated paramedical professions says the corresponding diploma/title is required and that European-holders may need authorisation to practise or an occasional-practice declaration.
open_issues.notes: Safe for route context only. It should not be promoted into a nurse-specific promise without a stronger France nursing source.
